features.name: Rage
features.description: Bonus Action; self; requires not wearing Heavy Armor. While raging: Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws; Resistance to bludgeoning/piercing/slashing damage; you can’t cast spells or maintain Concentration. Rage Damage: when you hit with a Strength-based weapon attack or Unarmed Strike, add +2 damage (Barbarian levels 1–8), +3 (levels 9–15), or +4 (levels 16–20). Duration: lasts until the end of your next turn; ends early if you don Heavy Armor or have the Incapacitated condition. To keep raging past your next turn, on that next turn do at least one of: make an attack roll against an enemy, force an enemy to make a saving throw, or take a Bonus Action to extend; each extension carries duration to the end of your following turn. Max duration 10 minutes. Uses: 2 rages at level 1; 3 at level 3; 4 at level 6; 5 at level 12; 6 at level 17. Regain 1 expended use on a Short Rest; regain all on a Long Rest.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: Passive; while not wearing any armor, your AC is 10 + Dex modifier + Con modifier. You can still use a Shield and benefit.

derived_effects.ac_formula_note: 10 + Dex modifier + Con modifier (no armor; shield allowed)

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Passive; you can use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial melee weapons you choose. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those chosen weapon kinds. Number of weapon kinds you can master: 2 (Barbarian levels 1–3), 3 (levels 4–9), 4 (levels 10–20).

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: Passive; you have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition. (This is your ‘sixth sense’ for dodging hazards.)

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: No action; when you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can choose to attack recklessly. Effect: you have Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, and attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

features.name: Primal Knowledge
features.description: Passive; gain proficiency in one additional Barbarian skill. While your Rage is active, when you make an ability check using Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival, you can treat it as a Strength check (using your Strength modifier) instead of its normal ability.

features.name: Barbarian Subclass
features.description: Choose a Barbarian subclass. You gain its features at levels 3, 6, 10, and 14.

features.name: Weapon Mastery Improvement
features.description: You learn one additional Weapon Mastery.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

features.name: Fast Movement
features.description: Passive; while you aren’t wearing Heavy Armor, your Speed increases by 10 feet.

derived_effects.condition_note: Applies while not wearing Heavy Armor

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: Passive; you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

features.name: Instinctive Pounce
features.description: No extra action; when you enter your Rage (as part of the same Bonus Action), you can move up to half your Speed immediately.

features.name: Brutal Strike
features.description: Trigger: on your turn, after you choose Reckless Attack, you may forgo Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll (the roll must not have Disadvantage). If that attack hits, it deals +1d10 extra damage of the same type and you apply one Brutal Strike effect: (1) Forceful Blow—push the target 15 ft straight away, then you may move up to half your Speed straight toward it without provoking Opportunity Attacks; or (2) Hamstring Blow—reduce the target’s Speed by 15 ft until the start of your next turn (a creature can be affected by only one Hamstring at a time).

features.name: Weapon Mastery Improvement
features.description: You learn one additional Weapon Mastery.

features.name: Relentless Rage
features.description: Trigger (no action required); if you drop to 0 HP while your Rage is active and don’t die outright, make a DC 10 Constitution save. On a success, your HP becomes twice your Barbarian level instead of 0. Each time you use this feature after the first (before you rest), the DC increases by 5; the DC resets to 10 when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: Passive upgrade; you add new Brutal Strike effect options: (1) Staggering Blow—target has Disadvantage on its next saving throw and can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn; (2) Sundering Blow—before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains +5 (only one Sundering bonus can apply to a roll).

features.name: Persistent Rage
features.description: Passive + trigger; your Rage can last the full 10 minutes without needing to extend it round-by-round. It still ends early if you don Heavy Armor or have the Unconscious condition. In addition, when you roll Initiative you can regain all expended Rage uses; once you do, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: Passive upgrade; your Brutal Strike extra damage becomes 2d10, and when you use Brutal Strike you can apply two different Brutal Strike effects to the same hit.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.description: Passive; if your total for a Strength check or a Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use your Strength score in place of the total.

features.name: Primal Champion
features.description: Passive; your Strength and Constitution scores each increase by 4, and their maximums become 25.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: Trigger: while your Rage is active, if you use Reckless Attack, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. Extra damage = roll a number of d6 equal to your Rage Damage bonus; damage type matches the weapon or Unarmed Strike.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: Passive; while your Rage is active, you are immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions. If you are Charmed or Frightened when you enter Rage, that condition ends on you immediately.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: Reaction; trigger: you take damage from a creature within 5 ft. Effect: make one melee attack against that creature (weapon or Unarmed Strike).

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: Bonus Action; 30 ft emanation; choose creatures in the area. Each chosen creature makes a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + Proficiency Bonus). On a fail: Frightened for 1 minute; it repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending on a success. Uses: 1 per Long Rest, but after using it you can restore your use by expending 1 Rage use (no action required).

features.name: Animal Speaker
features.description: Passive; you can cast Beast Sense and Speak with Animals, but only as rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Rage of the Wilds
features.description: Trigger: whenever you activate your Rage, choose one option: (1) Bear—while raging, you have Resistance to all damage types except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant; (2) Eagle—when you Rage you can Disengage and Dash as part of the same Bonus Action, and while raging you can take a Bonus Action to Disengage and Dash; (3) Wolf—while raging, your allies have Advantage on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 ft of you.

features.name: Aspect of the Wilds
features.description: Passive; choose one option (changeable on a Long Rest): Owl—Darkvision 60 ft (or +60 ft if you already have it); Panther—Climb Speed equal to your Speed; Salmon—Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

features.name: Nature Speaker
features.description: Passive; you can cast Commune with Nature, but only as a ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Power of the Wilds
features.description: Trigger: whenever you activate your Rage, choose one option: (1) Falcon—while raging and wearing no armor, you gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed; (2) Lion—while raging, enemies within 5 ft of you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you (or another Barbarian with Lion active); (3) Ram—while raging, when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee attack, you can cause it to be Prone.

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description: Trigger: when you activate your Rage, you gain Temporary HP equal to your Barbarian level. Also, at the start of each of your turns while raging, you can choose another creature within 10 ft to gain Temporary HP = roll a number of d6 equal to your Rage Damage bonus; any Temp HP from this feature vanish when your Rage ends.

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: Reaction; trigger: a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 ft while your Rage is active. The target makes a Strength save (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + Proficiency Bonus). On a fail: it is teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 ft of you (or the nearest unoccupied space you can see), and after it teleports you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Battering Boots
features.description: Passive; during your turn, your reach is increased by 10 ft with melee weapons that have the Heavy or Versatile property. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can also apply the Push or Topple mastery property in addition to whatever other mastery property you’re using with that weapon.

features.name: Travel along the Tree
features.description: Teleportation; when you activate your Rage, and also as a Bonus Action while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. Once per Rage, you can extend one such teleport to 150 ft; when you do, you can also bring up to six willing creatures within 10 ft of you, each arriving in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 ft of your destination.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: While your Rage is active, on each of your turns the first creature you hit with a weapon attack or an Unarmed Strike takes extra damage = 1d6 + half your Barbarian level (rounded down). Choose Radiant or Necrotic each time you deal this extra damage.

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: You have a pool of four d12s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll’s total. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: Once per Rage, if you fail a saving throw while your Rage is active, you can reroll it. You must use the new roll.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: Bonus Action; unleash a divine battle cry. Up to 10 other creatures of your choice within 60 ft that can hear you gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

features.name: Rage of the Gods
features.description: While your Rage is active, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. In addition, at the start of each of your turns while raging, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 ft; it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or take Radiant damage equal to 1d6 + half your Barbarian level (rounded down).
